default.summary: Electricity price volatility varies by market. Long-term PPAs and on-site renewables can reduce exposure. Check regional wholesale and utility rates.

CA.summary: CAISO: Some price volatility; duck curve and renewables shift patterns. Long-term contracts and behind-the-meter solar help stabilize costs.

TX.summary: ERCOT: Higher wholesale volatility; real-time and day-ahead swings. Secure long-term PPAs or hedge to manage price risk for H2 production.

NM.summary: SPP/PNM: Moderate volatility. Solar resource supports PPA stability. Lock in terms where possible.

AZ.summary: Desert Southwest: Strong solar reduces net exposure; utility and wholesale terms vary. PPAs common for large loads.

NV.summary: NV Energy / WECC: Renewable growth and contracts can stabilize costs. Negotiate or procure long-term supply for H2.

WA.summary: BPA and utilities: Hydro-dominated; generally more stable prices. Industrial rates and contracts vary.

OR.summary: BPA, PGE, PacifiCorp: Mix of hydro and markets. Long-term industrial contracts improve predictability.

WY.summary: SPP / PacifiCorp: Wind and coal mix; price stability moderate. Contract terms matter for large loads.

UT.summary: PacifiCorp: Diverse resource mix; industrial rates and PPAs can provide stability.

CO.summary: Xcel, SPP: Growing renewables; PPA market active. Lock in supply for predictable H2 economics.

IA.summary: MISO: Wind-heavy; wholesale volatility exists. Long-term PPAs widely used for industrial offtake.

OK.summary: SPP: Wind and gas; some volatility. Secure contracts for base-load H2 economics.

KS.summary: SPP: Wind and thermal; moderate stability. PPAs and utility agreements help.

IL.summary: MISO/PJM: Mixed markets; volatility by zone. Long-term contracts and renewables reduce risk.

NY.summary: NYISO: Some volatility; policy and renewables shifting stack. Industrial and large-load contracts available.

PA.summary: PJM: Wholesale volatility; capacity and energy markets. Hedge or contract for stable H2 input costs.

OH.summary: PJM: Industrial rates and market exposure vary. Long-term supply agreements improve stability.

FL.summary: FPL, Duke: Regulated utilities; relatively stable retail and industrial rates. Contract terms key.

LA.summary: Entergy, SPP: Industrial corridor; some gas-driven volatility. Long-term agreements common for large users.

ND.summary: MISO/SPP: Wind and coal; moderate price stability. Industrial offtake contracts available.

NE.summary: SPP: Wind and thermal mix; PPA market supports stable pricing for large loads.

MT.summary: NorthWestern, SPP: Hydro and wind; generally stable. Contract for firm supply where needed.
